compiled_state.title: As a developer, a large commit that doesn't match a refactor pattern gets a full-coverage review with all agents upgraded to opus
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Implement the non-refactor fallback branch in REVIEW-WORKFLOW-HUGE.md. When pattern confidence is below threshold (or <7 eligible files were available for sampling), emit MODEL_OVERRIDE: opus, route to the standard review classifier, and override all tier sub-agents to use opus regardless of classifier tier selection. The final findings file must be identical in schema to the standard review path output.

**Why**: Ensures large commits that aren't clean refactors still get thorough review. Large non-refactor commits are the highest-risk commits for the classifier to underestimate — MODEL_OVERRIDE compensates by guaranteeing opus-level analysis regardless of how the classifier scores the diff.

**Scope**:
- IN: MODEL_OVERRIDE: opus signal emission before dispatch, routing to standard review classifier, model override enforcement for all tier sub-agents (light→light-opus, standard→standard-opus, deep→deep-opus), findings schema identical to standard review path output
- OUT: confirmed refactor path (Story S3), SIZE_WARNING changes (Story S5)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, when a large commit fails to meet >=5/7 pattern consensus (or is treated as low confidence due to <7 eligible files), the workflow emits MODEL_OVERRIDE: opus before dispatching any sub-agent
  ← Satisfies: "Non-refactor large commits return to the standard review path; before dispatching any sub-agent the workflow emits a MODEL_OVERRIDE: opus signal"
- When this story is complete, all tier sub-agents dispatched on the fallback path use the opus model regardless of classifier tier selection, and the final findings file is identical in schema to the standard review path output
  ← Satisfies: "All tier sub-agents are instantiated with the opus model regardless of classifier tier; the final findings file is identical in schema to the standard review path output"
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Architecture] MODEL_OVERRIDE must be reconciled with REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md's model immutability rule ("Model is non-negotiable: The model: field in each named agent's definition is authoritative. Do NOT override it at dispatch time"). The implementation must either: (a) create opus-variant agent files (code-reviewer-light-opus.md, code-reviewer-standard-opus.md, code-reviewer-deep-*-opus.md) that are dispatched instead of the standard agents when MODEL_OVERRIDE is active, or (b) document a formal, explicit exception to the model immutability rule in REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md. This architectural decision must be resolved before implementation begins.
- [Testing] MODEL_OVERRIDE enforcement must be verifiable — the override signal and resulting model selection must be observable in workflow output, not just inferred from agent behavior
- [Maintainability] MODEL_OVERRIDE signal should be consistent with any existing model-override patterns in the codebase; check for prior uses before defining a new pattern

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
